site.name: HERA Research Hub
site.subtitle: MBIE programme prototype
site.description: A shared hub for HERA research software, case studies, and citable outputs.

home.title: A shared hub for HERA research software, case studies, and citable outputs.
home.description: This concept website gives each working group a consistent place to present code, software, datasets, papers, and industrial case studies with GitHub version control, Zenodo archiving, DOI links, and short release summaries.

featured.title: Featured hub prototype
featured.description: HERA MBIE research outputs web hub concept

highlights.title: One catalogue
highlights.description: Searchable records for software, case studies, reports, and papers.

highlights.title: Citable releases
highlights.description: Each GitHub release is archived on Zenodo with version metadata and DOI.

highlights.title: Team ownership
highlights.description: Working groups keep their own repositories while using a shared presentation standard.

highlights.title: Courses and training
highlights.description: Short learning modules help partners use the tools and understand research methods.

modules.title: Projects
modules.description: Explore research themes, participating institutions, and each team's areas of work.

modules.title: Resources
modules.description: Browse software, notebooks, datasets, papers, and industry case studies.

modules.title: Courses
modules.description: Find learning modules for researchers, students, and industry partners.

modules.title: Publication Pathway
modules.description: Follow the shared process from a working repository to a citable release.

projects.title: Research themes and university teams
projects.description: Each group has a public-facing project record with a summary, contributors, key outputs, repository links, DOI records, and current release status.

items.title: AI and Engineering
items.summary: Machine learning and decision-support methods for steel engineering applications.
topics: Machine learning for steel design workflows

items.title: Smart Structures
items.summary: Data-driven monitoring and digital-twin workflows for structural systems.
topics: Sensor data pipelines and digital twins

items.title: Manufacturing and Fabrication
items.summary: Automation and quality-assurance resources for fabrication workflows.

items.title: Sustainability and Resilience
items.summary: Research supporting lower-emissions and more resilient steel design decisions.

resources.title: Catalogue of research outputs
resources.description: Software, notebooks, datasets, papers, and case studies are presented with release metadata in one place.

items.title: Steel Connection Digital Twin Toolkit
items.description: Python package and example workflows for calibrating connection models from laboratory and sensor data.

items.title: AI-Assisted Member Design Examples
items.description: Reproducible Jupyter notebooks showing model training, validation, and design interpretation for structural engineering tasks.

items.title: Bridge Monitoring Demonstrator
items.description: Industrial case study summarising data collection, model assumptions, validation results, and adoption pathway for infrastructure monitoring.

items.title: Responsible AI for Steel Engineering Workflows
items.description: Paper record linking publication, source code, trained model release, citation text, and reproducibility checklist.

items.title: Fabrication QA Image Pipeline
items.description: Prototype computer vision pipeline for documenting weld quality checks and producing traceable evidence packs.

items.title: Low-Carbon Design Options Catalogue
items.description: Industry-facing summaries connecting lifecycle models, design options, assumptions, and supporting technical reports.

courses.title: Training for researchers, students, and industry partners
courses.description: Course pages package workshops, notebooks, slides, example datasets, assessment material, and links to relevant research outputs.

items.title: Research Software and Reproducible Releases
items.description: Practical guidance for GitHub repositories, version tags, documentation, licensing, Zenodo archiving, DOI citation, and release notes.

items.title: AI for Steel Engineering Applications
items.description: Introductory and applied material on data preparation, model validation, uncertainty, responsible AI, and engineering interpretation.

items.title: Smart Structures and Monitoring Data
items.description: Training for sensor data workflows, digital twin calibration, data quality, field deployment records, and industry-facing case study reporting.

process.title: From research output to citable release
process.description: A shared checklist helps every team publish outputs in a consistent, professional, and reproducible format.

steps.title: Create a GitHub repository
steps.description: Include README, licence, contributors, installation notes, and a minimal working example.

steps.title: Version the output
steps.description: Tag stable releases using semantic versioning for software or date-based versions for reports.

steps.title: Archive with Zenodo
steps.description: Connect GitHub to Zenodo, archive each release, and record the DOI and citation text.

steps.title: Publish a hub record
steps.description: Add the short description, team, version, DOI, related paper, and maintenance status.

people.title: Programme and theme leaders
people.description: Approved biographies, contact routes, institutional affiliations, and responsibility areas can be maintained here.

items.name: Prototype Maintainer
items.description: Maintains the concept hub structure, prototype records, and publication workflow examples.

items.name: AI and Engineering Theme Lead
items.description: Owns software, model governance, notebooks, and reproducible AI workflows.

items.name: Smart Structures Theme Lead
items.description: Owns monitoring datasets, digital twin tools, validation plans, and sensor case studies.

items.name: Industry Reference Lead
items.description: Connects research outputs with member needs, deployment examples, and implementation guidance.

about.title: Connecting research outputs with New Zealand heavy engineering impact.
paragraphs: This prototype positions HERA as the coordinating body for a transparent, citable, and industry-readable research programme.
The final website can use approved HERA branding, confirmed working group names, live repository data, real DOI records, and maintained programme contacts.

principles.title: Transparent
principles.description: Research outputs are clearly described, versioned, and linked to their supporting evidence.

principles.title: Citable
principles.description: Stable releases connect repository records with archival DOI references.

principles.title: Industry-readable
principles.description: Technical work is accompanied by concise summaries, practical context, and adoption guidance.
